paths.@rayspec/db/testing: Raw, tenant-UNSCOPED Drizzle handle (makeDb/makeDbWithSchema). Shipped request/orchestration code must hold only a TenantDb via forTenant(...) from @rayspec/db. The /testing subpath is for the composition root + test seam (test-support/** and *.test.ts) only.

Raw, tenant-UNSCOPED Drizzle handle (makeDb/makeDbWithSchema) + the gate-only Set mutators. Shipped request/orchestration code must hold only a TenantDb via forTenant(...) from @rayspec/db. The /testing subpath is for the composition root + test seam (test-support/** and *.test.ts) only. (Biome overrides REPLACE — not merge — a rule's options, so this ban is RE-DECLARED here alongside the makeDb + composition bans; without it this scoped root would silently lose the packages/**/src /testing ban.)
paths.@rayspec/db/composition: registerProductStores/sealProductStores is the SANCTIONED product-store registrar (the boot-time door onto the tenant chokepoint Set). A scoped request/orchestration root is NOT a composition root — it must NEVER register product tables (that could defeat deny-by-default). Only the boot composition root (@rayspec/server/src) + the CLI deploy entrypoint (@rayspec/cli/src) may import it. This mirrors the tenant-chokepoint gate's registerProductStores token ban.

@rayspec/db.message: makeDb is the raw, tenant-UNSCOPED production handle factory, now on the MAIN @rayspec/db surface (the boot composition root needs it). It may be named ONLY in the composition root (@rayspec/server/src, NOT in this scoped-root override). Scoped request/orchestration code must hold a TenantDb via forTenant(db, tenantId) — the `Db` TYPE + forTenant from @rayspec/db remain allowed. This is the 2nd barrier behind the import-source-agnostic tenant-chokepoint grep gate (scripts/check-tenant-chokepoint.mjs), restoring the module-boundary double-guard the /testing-only ban no longer provides for makeDb.
